evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG/DTM debug requester via tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i or simulation DTM/JTAG
evidence_slots.operation: issue DMI read/write operations, debug requests, and debug reset/control effects

evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMI/JTAG operations reaching protected debug controls should be gated by explicit authorization such as jtag_unlock, authenticated DMI state, lifecycle/testmode restriction, or debug_mode_i policy before dm_top control outputs are driven.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag receives hash inputs and outputs jtag_unlock, but debug_req/debug_req_valid are visibly connected directly from i_dmi_jtag, or SimDTM in simulation, into dm_top. The provided evidence does not show jtag_unlock gating dmi_req_valid_i, dmi_req_i, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, or debug_req_o. dm_master_gnt, dm_master_r_valid, and dm_master_r_rdata are tied to zero.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top -> ndmreset_o/dmactive_o/debug_req_o and dm_slave path; simulation path SimDTM -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top.
evidence_slots.state_condition: RISCV_FESVR_SIM selects SimDTM; otherwise dmi_jtag is used. RISCV_JTAG_SIM can introduce SimJTAG. testmode_i is passed into dmi_jtag, but no visible top-level authorization gate is applied to DMI request flow.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthenticated or insufficiently authenticated JTAG/DTM may activate debug, request hart debug entry, or drive debug reset/control if dmi_jtag does not internally enforce authorization.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Visible top-level RTL connects DMI/JTAG debug requests into dm_top without an explicit top-level jtag_unlock/debug authorization gate. dm_master memory-master responses are tied off, reducing memory-master impact, but debug control outputs remain reachable if dmi_jtag itself does not enforce authorization.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate depends on whether dmi_jtag internally suppresses unauthorized DMI; no such internal evidence was provided.

evidence_slots.subject: DMA engine as bus master
evidence_slots.operation: read source and write destination DMA transfer
evidence_slots.object: memory regions reachable through configured source/destination addresses
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Each current source and destination address should be checked against PMP for the correct access type before transfer; stale pmp_allow_reg or special flags must not authorize changed addresses/parameters.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: CTRL_CHECK_LOAD iterates source addresses with ACCESS_READ, but pmp_allow_new includes an override `(we_flag && length_d == 3)`. CTRL_LOAD asserts valid load, then sets pmp_check_ctr_new = length_d, pmp_addr_new = 0, and pmp_allow_new = 1 before store checks. CTRL_CHECK_STORE checks destination addresses with ACCESS_WRITE but sets pmp_allow_new = pmp_data_allow, not an accumulated allow across all beats, and final transition depends on pmp_allow_reg && pmp_data_allow.
evidence_slots.path: DMA config registers -> dma save_inputs -> pmp_addr_reg/pmp_access_type_reg -> pmp pmp_data_allow -> pmp_allow_reg -> valid_reg/valid_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: Inputs saved when dma_ctrl_reg is CTRL_IDLE or CTRL_DONE. Source checked in CTRL_CHECK_LOAD, destination in CTRL_CHECK_STORE. pmp_allow_reg reset/reinitialized during CTRL_LOAD.
evidence_slots.impact: Special-case override or non-accumulated destination checks may permit DMA transfer signaling despite a denied beat, stale pmp_allow state, or policy bypass under we_flag/length_d==3.

rtl_evidence.detail: pmp_access_type_reg and pmp_allow_reg/en declarations.
DMA input parameters saved while idle/done.
source check sets ACCESS_READ and pmp_allow_new with we_flag && length_d == 3 override.
CTRL_LOAD asserts valid and resets pmp_allow_new to 1.
destination check sets ACCESS_WRITE and pmp_allow_new = pmp_data_allow.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full FSM initialization, abort behavior, pmp instantiation wiring, and valid_o consumer semantics are truncated.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: DMA includes PMP checks for source reads and destination writes, but visible sequencing contains bypass/stale-authorization hazards: a we_flag/length special-case can force source pmp_allow_new, and pmp_allow_reg is reset to 1 after load before destination checks.

evidence_slots.subject: memory access requester using PMP check
evidence_slots.operation: authorize memory access based on PMP entry match
evidence_slots.expected_guard: PMP matching should correctly implement OFF/TOR/NA4/NAPOT ranges, first-match priority, access-type/privilege semantics, and default deny for non-M no-match.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: pmp.sv initializes allow_o=0, iterates entries in order, applies entries in S/U or locked M mode, checks `(access_type_i & conf_i[i].access_type) == access_type_i`, breaks on first match, allows M-mode no-match, and denies non-M no-match. pmp_entry implements TOR as prev <= addr < current. For NA4/NAPOT it computes size, then `mask = '1 << size`, `base = (conf_addr_i << 2) & mask`, and matches `(addr_i & mask) == base`, which appears to test only one shifted bit rather than a full NAPOT/NA4 range mask.
evidence_slots.state_condition: If NR_ENTRIES==0, allow_o is tied to 1. If no entry matches and priv_lvl_i==M, allow_o is 1; otherwise 0. Locked entries apply to M mode.
evidence_slots.impact: Incorrect NA4/NAPOT matching could grant or deny accesses outside/inside configured PMP ranges, undermining DMA/core memory protection.

rtl_evidence.detail: first-match allow logic and default no-match behavior.
NR_ENTRIES==0 default allows all access.
TOR match is lower inclusive, upper exclusive.
NA4/NAPOT size/mask/base/match logic uses `mask = '1 << size`.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: PMP allow priority/no-match logic is mostly architectural, but pmp_entry NAPOT/NA4 matching appears incorrect: mask is computed as `'1 << size` and base uses bitwise AND with that mask, which does not form a range mask and can mis-match addresses.

evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level declares reset lines rst_1..rst_13 and exposes reset NoC ports. DMA wrapper receives rst_8 and uses reset condition ~(rst_ni && ~rst_8) to clear core_lock_reg/start/length/source/dest registers. The reset-control wrapper/decode and acc_ctrl gate were not provided, so authorization for asserting rst_N and whether rst_6/rst_9 clear acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl cannot be determined.
evidence_slots.path: buf_ariane_rst_noc2_* -> reset NoC/AXI-lite path -> reset wrapper/control registers -> rst_1..rst_13 -> peripheral wrappers including DMA rst_8
evidence_slots.state_condition: DMA configuration resets on either global reset or rst_8 assertion. ACCT uses rst_6; REGLK uses rst_9; many security wrappers use their corresponding rst_N.
evidence_slots.impact: If reset control is not privileged or can reset lock/policy state, software may bypass locks by resetting protected peripherals such as DMA or policy wrappers.

rtl_evidence.detail: top-level reset NoC ports.
rst_1..rst_13 reset signals declared.
rst_8 input declared.
rst_8 participates in reset condition and clears DMA registers.
top-level passes rst_8 to dma_wrapper.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Reset wrapper source/decode, access-control gate for reset writes, and reset effects on acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Reset-control NoC interface and per-peripheral reset outputs are present, and DMA rst_8 clears DMA configuration/core_lock, but reset wrapper write privilege and whether protected policy state can be cleared are not evidenced.

evidence_slots.subject: debug/JTAG/test requester
evidence_slots.operation: read/write/override protected state through debug or test path
evidence_slots.object: protected peripheral state, DMA configuration, fuse/crypto data, acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl
evidence_slots.expected_guard: testmode_i, SimDTM/SimJTAG, debug_mode_i, and jtag_unlock paths should not bypass normal acc_ctrl/reglk/PMP checks unless simulation-only or lifecycle-authorized.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag receives testmode_i and drives debug_req/debug_req_valid toward dm_top without visible top-level jtag_unlock gate. SimDTM/SimJTAG branches can drive debug request paths under preprocessor conditions. reglk_wrapper receives jtag_unlock, and AES/RSA wrappers receive debug_mode_i, but the provided evidence does not show that these debug/test signals are restricted to authorized lifecycle state or that any writes use normal access-control/lock gates.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG/testmode/SimDTM/SimJTAG -> debug_req/debug_req_valid/dm_top and jtag_unlock/debug_mode_i -> reglk/security wrappers -> protected policy/security state
evidence_slots.state_condition: RISCV_FESVR_SIM and RISCV_JTAG_SIM alter debug source. testmode_i goes into dmi_jtag. jtag_unlock goes into reglk_wrapper. debug_mode_i enters AES0/RSA and likely other crypto wrappers. DMA protection relies on acct_ctrl_i/PMP, with we_flag inputs also present.
evidence_slots.impact: Debug/test modes may provide alternate authorization or bypass channels to policy locks and secret-capable peripherals if wrappers interpret jtag_unlock/debug_mode_i/we_flag as override signals without lifecycle confinement.

rtl_evidence.detail: SimDTM branch drives debug request/response.
dmi_jtag receives testmode_i and exposes jtag_unlock_o while driving DMI request signals.
SimJTAG/physical JTAG alternatives drive tck/tms/trst/tdi/tdo paths.
AES0 wrapper receives debug_mode_i with acc_ctrl/reglk inputs.
RSA wrapper receives debug_mode_i with acc_ctrl/reglk inputs.
reglk_wrapper receives jtag_unlock per tool observation.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Wrapper internals for reglk/security peripherals and preprocessor/lifecycle build constraints.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Visible debug/test signals cross into protected policy/security assets. jtag_unlock is not shown gating debug entry but is supplied to reglk_wrapper; debug_mode_i is passed to crypto wrappers. These paths are not proven confined to lifecycle/simulation modes and may bypass normal acc_ctrl/reglk checks depending on wrapper internals.

13: // Description: purely combinatorial PMP unit (with extraction for more complex configs such as NAPOT)
14: 
15: module pmp #(
16:     parameter int unsigned XLEN = 32,       // rv64: 64
17:     parameter int unsigned PMP_LEN = 32,    // rv64: 54
18:     parameter int unsigned NR_ENTRIES = 4
19: ) (
20:     // Input
21:     input logic [XLEN-1:0] addr_i,
22:     input riscv::pmp_access_t access_type_i,
23:     input riscv::priv_lvl_t priv_lvl_i,
24:     // Configuration
25:     input logic [NR_ENTRIES-1:0][PMP_LEN-1:0] conf_addr_i,
26:     input riscv::pmpcfg_t [NR_ENTRIES-1:0] conf_i,
27:     // Output
28:     output logic allow_o
29: );
30:     // if there are no PMPs we can always grant the access.
31:     if (NR_ENTRIES > 0) begin : gen_pmp
32:         logic [NR_ENTRIES-1:0] match;
33: 
34:         for (genvar i = 0; i < NR_ENTRIES; i++) begin
35:             pmp_entry #(
36:                 .XLEN    ( XLEN    ),
37:                 .PMP_LEN ( PMP_LEN )
38:             ) i_pmp_entry(
39:                 .addr_i           ( addr_i                         ),
40:                 .conf_addr_i      ( conf_addr_i[i]                 ),
41:                 .conf_addr_prev_i ( i == 0 ? '0 : conf_addr_i[i-1] ),
42:                 .conf_addr_mode_i ( conf_i[i].addr_mode            ),
43:                 .match_o          ( match[i]                       )
44:             );
45:         end
46: 
47:         always_comb begin
48:             int i;
49:             
50:             allow_o = 1'b0;
51:             for (i = 0; i < NR_ENTRIES; i++) begin
52:                 // either we are in S or U mode or the config is locked in which
53:                 // case it also applies in M mode
54:                 if (priv_lvl_i != riscv::PRIV_LVL_M || conf_i[i].locked) begin
55:                     if (match[i]) begin
56:                         if ((access_type_i & conf_i[i].access_type) != access_type_i) allow_o = 1'b0;
57:                         else allow_o = 1'b1;
58:                         break;
59:                     end
60:                 end
61:             end
62:             if (i == NR_ENTRIES) begin // no PMP entry matched the address
63:                 // allow all accesses from M-mode for no pmp match
64:                 if (priv_lvl_i == riscv::PRIV_LVL_M) allow_o = 1'b1;
65:                 // disallow accesses for all other modes
66:                 else allow_o = 1'b0;
67:             end
68:         end
69:     end else assign allow_o = 1'b1;
70: 
71:     `ifdef FORMAL
72:     always @(*) begin
73:         if(priv_lvl_i == riscv::PRIV_LVL_M) begin
74:             static logic no_locked = 1'b1;
75:             for (int i = 0; i < NR_ENTRIES; i++) begin
76:                 if (conf_i[i].locked && conf_i[i].addr_mode != riscv::OFF) begin
77:                     no_locked &= 1'b0;
78:                 end else no_locked &= 1'b1;
79:             end
80:             
81:             if (no_locked == 1'b1) assert(allow_o == 1'b1);
82:         end
83:     end
84:     `endif
85: endmodule

15: module pmp_entry #(

17:     parameter int unsigned PMP_LEN = 54

20:     input logic [XLEN-1:0] addr_i,
21:

51:                 end else begin
52:                     assert(addr_i < (conf_addr_i << 2) || addr_i >= (conf_addr_prev_i << 2));
53:                 end
54:                 `endif
55:             end
56:             riscv::NA4, riscv::NAPOT:   begin
57:                 logic [XLEN-1:0] base;
58:                 logic [XLEN-1:0] mask;
59:                 int unsigned size;
60:

63:                     // use the extracted trailing ones

65:                 end
